questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
A conducting disc of conductivity $\sigma$ has a radius a and thickness $t$. If the magnetic field $B$ is applied in a direction perpendicular to the plane of the disc, changes with time at the rate of $\frac{d B}{d t}=\alpha$. Calculate the power dissipated (in mW ) in the disc due to the induced current.
[Take : $\alpha=2 \mathrm{~T} / \mathrm{s}, \mathrm{a}=1 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{t}=2 \mathrm{~mm}$ and $\sigma=\frac{4}{\pi} \times 10^8 \Omega^{-1} \mathrm{~m}^{-1}$ ]
questions.solution: $\mathrm{P}=\frac{\pi t \sigma}{2} \alpha^2 \int_0^a r^3 d r=\frac{\pi t \sigma a^4}{8} \alpha^2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A conducting rod Pq is sliding on two parallel conducting rods with constant velocity in a uniform magnetic field of induction $\mathrm{B}=2$ $\mathrm{Wb} / \mathrm{m}^2$ as shown in the figure. The rods are connected with a circuit. What should be velocity of rod Pq (in $\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$ ) if current in $2 \Omega$ resistor is 0.1 A ?
questions.solution:
Current is $2 \Omega=0.1 \mathrm{~A}$
current in circuit $=0.2 \mathrm{~A}$
equivalent resistance about $A B=5 \Omega$
$\mathrm{e}=\mid \mathrm{R}=1$ volts
and also
$\mathrm{e}=\mathrm{B} \ell \mathrm{V}$
$\therefore \mathrm{V}=\frac{\mathrm{e}}{\mathrm{B} \ell}=\frac{1}{2 \times 0.1}=5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two parallel conducting rails are connected to a source of emf E and internal resistance r. Another conducting rod of length $\ell$ having negligible resistance lies at rest and can slide without friction over the rails. A uniform magnetic field B is applied perpendicular to the plane of the rails. At $t=0$, the rod is pulled along the rails by applying a force $F$. The velocity of the rod is observed to be $v=v_0 \cos (\omega t)$ then find the power (in watt) spent by the force over 1 cycle. (Given $\mathrm{B}=2$ Tesla, $\mathrm{r}=2 \times 10^{-4} \Omega, \mathrm{v}_0=2 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}, \ell=1 \mathrm{~cm}$ )
questions.solution:
Force on the rod
$F-F_m=m a$
$F-\frac{B \ell}{r}(B / V-E)=m a$
$F+\frac{B \ell E}{r}-\frac{B^2 \ell^2 V}{r}=\frac{m d V}{d t}$
solving
$F=-m V_0 \omega \sin (\omega t)+\frac{B^2 \ell^2}{r} V_0 \cos \omega t-\frac{B \ell E}{r}$
Power expended by force is
$F V=-m V_0^2 \omega \sin (\omega t) \cos (\omega t)+\frac{B^2 \ell^2}{r} V_0^2 \cos ^2 \omega t-\frac{B \ell E}{r} V_0 \cos \omega t$
Its average over the cycle is
$\frac{\mathrm{B}^2 \ell^2 \mathrm{~V}_0^2}{2 \mathrm{r}}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A uniform but time varying magnetic field $B=C-K t$, where $K \& C$ are positive constants and $t$ is time (in seconds), is applied perpendicular to the plane of a circular loop of radius ' $a$ ' and resistance R. Find the total charge (in coulomb) that will pass through any point of the loop by the time B becomes zero. [Given $\mathrm{a}=2 \mathrm{~m}, \mathrm{R}=\pi \Omega, \mathrm{C}=2 \mathrm{~T}$ ]
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \phi=\vec{B} \cdot \vec{A} ; \varepsilon=-\frac{d \phi}{d t}=K \pi a^2 ; i=\frac{\varepsilon}{R}=\frac{K a^2}{R} \text { and at } \mathrm{t}=\mathrm{T} ; \mathrm{B}=0 \Rightarrow 0=C-K T \Rightarrow T=\frac{C}{K} \\ & q=i \times T=\frac{\pi a^2 C}{R}=8 C\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A non-conducting ring of radius R having uniformly distributed charge $Q$ starts rotating about $x-x^{\prime}$ axis passing through diameter with an angular acceleration $\alpha$ as shown in the figure. Another small conducting ring having radius a ( $\lt \lt R$ ) is kept fixed at the centre of bigger ring is such a way that axis $x x^{\prime}$ is passing through its centre and perpendicular to its plane. If the resistance of small ring is $r=1 \Omega$, find the induced current in it in ampere. (Given $\mathrm{q}=\frac{16 \times 10^2}{u_0} \mathrm{C}, \mathrm{R}=1 \mathrm{~m}, \mathrm{a}=0.1 \mathrm{~m}, \alpha=8 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}^2$)
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & d q=\frac{q}{2 \pi R} \cdot R d \theta=\frac{q}{2 \pi} \cdot d \theta \\ & d i=\frac{d q}{T}=\frac{q d \theta \omega}{2 \pi 2 \pi} \\ & d i=\frac{q \omega}{4 \pi^2} \cdot d \theta \\ & d B=\frac{\mu_0 d i(R \sin \theta)^2}{2 R^3} \\ & \int d B=\int_0^\pi \frac{\mu_0 \sin ^2 \theta}{2 R}\left(\frac{q \omega}{4 \pi^2}\right) d \theta \\ & B=\frac{\mu_0 q \omega}{16 \pi R} \\ & \phi=B \pi a^2 \\ & \phi=\pi a^2 \cdot \frac{\mu_0 q \omega}{16 \pi R} \\ & \phi=\frac{\mu_0 q \omega a^2}{16 R} \\ & |\varepsilon|=\left|\frac{d \phi}{d t}\right| \\ & |\varepsilon|=\frac{\mu_0 q a^2}{16 R} \alpha \\ & =8 \text { volt } \\ & i=\frac{8}{1}=8 \mathrm{~A} .\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Two ideal solenoids of same dimensions. One is air cored with 600 turns while other is Aluminium cored with 200 turns (relative permeability of Aluminium is 3 ), are connected in a circuit as shown in the figure. The switch $S$ is closed at $t=0$. Find the ratio of potential difference across air-cored solenoid to that of Aluminium cored solenoid at any time $t$.

questions.q:
A circular conducting coil of radius 1 m is being heated by the change of magnetic field B→ passing perpendicular to the plane in which the coil is laid. The resistance of the coil is 2 μΩ. The magnetic field is slowly switched off such that its magnitude changes in time as  

B=4π×10-3 T1-t100

The energy dissipated by the coil before the magnetic field is switched off completely is E=______mJ.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The circuit shows a resistance $\mathrm{R}=0.01 \Omega$ and inductance $\mathrm{L}=3 \mathrm{mH}$ connected to a conducting rod PQ of length $\ell=2 \mathrm{~m}$ which can slide on a perfectly conducting circular arc of radius $\ell$ with its centre at P . Assume that friction \& gravity are absent and a constant uniform magnetic field $B=0.1 \mathrm{~T}$ exists as shown in the figure. At $\mathrm{t}=0$, the circuit is switched on and simultaneously an external torque is applied on the rod so that it rotates about $P$ with a constant angular velocity $\omega=2 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{sec}$. Find the magnitude of this torque (in $\mathrm{N}-\mathrm{m})$ at $\mathrm{t}=(0.3 \ln 2)$ second.
questions.solution:
The half of the maximum current is equal to
$\mathrm{i}_1=\frac{\mathrm{B} \omega \ell^2}{4 \mathrm{R}}$ at $\mathrm{t}=\frac{\mathrm{L}}{\mathrm{R}} \ln 2$
The torque at this instant $=\frac{\mathrm{B}^2 \omega \ell^4}{8 \mathrm{R}}=4 \mathrm{~N}-\mathrm{m}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
